mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains positive over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day return during months with rising FEDFUNDS is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate greater than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating household income expectations and corporate revenue slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE has risen greater than 0.3 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly elevated CPI inflation and federal funds rate squeeze corporate margins through higher input costs and debt service, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS shows a positive beta with 60-day returns in walk-forward OLS regression with p-value below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales signal strengthening consumer demand, driving near-term revenue beats and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during early-cycle recovery.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS momentum coincides with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of early-recovery months during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in industrial production indicates rising capacity utilization and operating leverage, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month growth in the top quartile fails to produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below its trailing 12-month average presages weaker construction activity and durable goods demand, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST crossing below its 12-month average is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll expansion and unemployment compression signal a tight labor market with robust wage-driven consumption, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond the sum of independent effects.
failure_condition: Joint positive PAYEMS and negative UNRATE changes fail to predict positive 20-day returns with Rank IC above 0.03 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4% sharply raises front-end Treasury yields and debt-service costs, triggering immediate de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS above 4% episodes coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of instances, or average return is not negative.
